ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers raw chicken meat broadly; no dedicated code exists for the wing portion without skin specifically. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for raw skinless chicken wing as a food product; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium raw skinless chicken wing with bone weighs approximately 55 g, yielding roughly 35–40 g of edible meat after cooking.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken wing, raw, without skin — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database; supplemented by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 05062 (Chicken, wing, meat only, raw)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board (HPB), Singapore; U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol) taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for chicken wing, raw, without skin (CR ID F11010034, confidence 0.95, reviewed). Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and additional micronutrients not reported in the HPB record were supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 05062 (Chicken, wing, meat only, raw), which is a closely matched approved secondary source. The HPB entry is an exact match; the USDA entry is an adapted match for the supplemented fields.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — food contains negligible carbohydrates
gi_source.source_match_notes: Chicken wing, raw, without skin contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g per HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. No GI value is assigned per Veritas q4 rules for negligible-carbohydrate foods. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (81 mg/100 g) with a favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio (194 mg K : 81 mg Na)
hypertension.guidance: Raw skinless chicken wing is a low-sodium lean protein suitable for hypertension management; however, patients should avoid high-sodium marinades such as soy sauce, oyster sauce, or buffalo sauce, and should season with herbs, garlic, or lemon juice instead to preserve the favourable sodium profile.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar; high protein (21.97 g/100 g) supports satiety and glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: With zero carbohydrate and zero sugar, raw skinless chicken wing has no glycaemic impact and is an excellent lean protein choice for blood glucose management in Type 2 Diabetes; patients should avoid sugar-based glazes (honey, teriyaki, BBQ sauce) during preparation and favour baking or grilling without batter to maintain this profile.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.97 g/100 g), low total fat (3.54 g/100 g), and low cholesterol (57 mg/100 g) relative to high protein content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Raw skinless chicken wing is a lean, low-saturated-fat protein source appropriate for cardiovascular disease management; patients should avoid deep-frying, which substantially increases total fat and generates pro-inflammatory advanced glycation end-products (AGEs), and should instead grill, bake, or steam the wings.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Absence of anti-inflammatory nutrients (omega-3, antioxidants, polyphenols, fibre); neutral profile does not actively support inflammation reduction
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Raw skinless chicken wing is not contraindicated in rheumatoid arthritis but provides no active anti-inflammatory benefit; patients should pair it with anti-inflammatory ingredients such as turmeric, ginger, or extra-virgin olive oil during preparation, and should strictly avoid deep-frying which generates AGEs that worsen joint inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken wing, raw, without skin is the uncooked wing portion of a chicken with the skin removed, leaving bone-in dark and white meat with a characteristic pinkish-red colour and moist, slightly firm texture. The wing comprises three sections — the drumette, the flat (wingette), and the tip — though the tip is often discarded; without skin, the exposed muscle tissue is lean and lower in fat than skin-on preparations. Per 100 g, it provides 126 kcal, 21.97 g protein, 3.54 g total fat (of which only 0.97 g is saturated), 0 g carbohydrate, 0 g sugar, 81 mg sodium, and 194 mg potassium. It is an excellent source of lean protein and B vitamins, particularly niacin (7.64 mg), vitamin B6 (0.53 mg), and vitamin B12 (0.27 mcg). Clinically, the zero-carbohydrate, zero-sugar profile makes it ideal for Type 2 Diabetes management; the low sodium and favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio support hypertension management; and the low saturated fat content is appropriate for cardiovascular disease prevention. It is halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Common cooking methods include grilling, baking, steaming, and stir-frying; deep-frying should be avoided for health-conscious preparations. It is widely used across global cuisines as a versatile protein ingredient.

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID F11010034, confidence 0.95, reviewed status). Fatty acid breakdown and additional micronutrients supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 05062 (Chicken, wing, meat only, raw), a closely matched approved secondary source. Both sources are on the approved list and the food identity is unambiguous.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as raw chicken wing without skin is a pure animal protein with no carbohydrate-containing components. Sugar_g is naturally zero for the same reason. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as animal muscle tissue contains no dietary fibre. Confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. Saturated fat (0.97 g), monounsaturated fat (1.29 g), polyunsaturated fat (0.76 g), trans fat (0.02 g), and micronutrients (vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g) sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 05062 (Chicken, wing, meat only, raw) as these fields were not reported in the HPB record. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0 g/100 g); gi_category set to not_applicable and glycemic_index set to null per Veritas q4 rules for negligible-carbohydrate foods. No SNOMED CT code assigned — no defensible match identified for raw skinless chicken wing as a food product in SNOMED CT. FoodEx2 code A00LC is a closest_parent match covering raw chicken meat broadly; no dedicated FoodEx2 code exists for the wing portion without skin specifically.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is the precise canonical English name from HPB. No local-language names are required as this is a generic global ingredient, not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish. G6 multilingual rule is satisfied.

Sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo (primary, exact match) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 05062 (secondary, adapted for supplemented fields). Zero carbohydrate justified in notes as naturally zero for animal muscle tissue. nutrient_source.source_match_type set to 'exact' reflecting the primary HPB source match, with full explanation in source_match_notes.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.0), sodium_mg (81.0), saturated_fat_g (0.97), sugar_g (0.0) all present as numeric values. Values are plausible and supported by HPB and USDA sources.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index set to null (not 0) as food contains negligible carbohydrates. gi_category set to not_applicable. glycemic_load_per_serving set to 0.0. gi_source.title, organization, url, and source_match_notes all document the rationale clearly. gi_source.url set to 'N/A' (plain string, not a URL) consistent with not-applicable status. Compliant with q4 low/zero-carbohydrate rule.

SNOMED CT omitted with honest gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters, explains what the food is, preparation context, taste/texture, clinical relevance (diabetes, hypertension, CVD), and key nutrients. Suitable for RAG and LLM training.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (low concern), Type 2 Diabetes (low concern, machine key type2_diabetes, human name Type 2 Diabetes), Cardiovascular Disease (low concern). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. Rheumatoid arthritis included as additional condition.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases array contains 8 English variants including canonical name, alternate phrasings, and component-level variants (wingette, drumette). aliases_by_language.english mirrors all 8 entries verbatim. All strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in flat aliases array — q8 consistency check passed. No mandatory local-language aliases required as this is a generic global ingredient.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Updated nutrient_source.title and nutrient_source.organization to reflect both HPB and USDA as sources; updated gi_source.url to 'N/A — food contains negligible carbohydrates'; set foodex2_match_type to 'adapted'.

Iteration 3: Changed foodex2_match_type from 'adapted' to 'closest_parent' — the only allowed non-exact value per the scoring model validator. Updated foodex2_gap_note accordingly. Changed gi_source.url from 'N/A — food contains negligible carbohydrates' to plain 'N/A' to avoid validator confusion. Set nutrient_source.source_match_type to 'exact' (was 'exact | adapted' compound string which is not a valid enum value) and moved the dual-source explanation into source_match_notes. Updated gi_source.title to be a plain descriptive string rather than a URL-like value.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Record passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8. Primary nutrient data sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo (exact match, confidence 0.95) and supplemented by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 05062 for comprehensive micronutrient and fatty acid profiles. nutrient_source.source_match_type corrected to valid enum value 'exact' with full dual-source explanation in source_match_notes. foodex2_match_type corrected to 'closest_parent' — a valid allowed value. gi_source.url set to plain 'N/A' for not-applicable GI status. glycemic_index correctly set to null with gi_category not_applicable per zero-carbohydrate rule. Zero macros (carbohydrate, sugar, fibre) justified as naturally zero for animal muscle tissue. All three mandatory health conditions present with specific clinical guidance. Alias coverage is comprehensive and internally consistent. No mandatory multilingual names required as this is not a cuisine-specific dish.
